pull.title: Use DeepGEMM BF16 for unquantized DeepEP LL MoE
pull.body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

This is a follow-up to #24906. The merged implementation enabled BF16 DeepEP dispatch for every `quant_config=None` MoE layer, which is too broad. This PR narrows the behavior to the target case: unquantized DeepEP low-latency MoE on CUDA, used by the Qwen3.5 NVFP4 MTP/NextN draft MoE path.

The Qwen3.5 NVFP4 main decode MoE can use `flashinfer_cutedsl + DeepEP`, but the MTP/NextN draft MoE is unquantized (`quant_config=None`). For this draft path, DeepEP low-latency dispatch should keep BF16 activations and let the existing unquantized `FusedMoE` path run the DeepGEMM BF16 masked runner.

This branch now includes #25525, where `flashinfer_cutedsl + DeepEP` is already migrated to `MoeRunner`, and #22822, which adds the DeepEP dispatcher output dtype mechanism. The narrow unquantized DeepEP LL case now uses `dispatcher_output_dtype=bf16` rather than the previous temporary `bf16_dispatch` key.

## Modifications

- Restrict BF16 DeepEP dispatch to unquantized low-latency DeepEP LL MoE on non-NPU/non-HIP backends, and only when the active MoE runner is `deep_gemm`, using #22822 `dispatcher_output_dtype=bf16`.
- Set `deprecate_flag=True` for that narrow case so execution delegates to the generic `FusedMoE` path: dispatch, `quant_method.apply`, DeepGEMM runner, and combine.
- Remove the local PyTorch `forward_unquantized_deepep_ll` fallback.
- Keep the #25525 `flashinfer_cutedsl + DeepEP` MoeRunner path intact; this PR does not reintroduce the old `forward_flashinfer_cutedsl` path.

## Accuracy Tests

Accuracy was checked with GSM8K using the matched harness: 5-shot, `max_tokens=16384`, `temperature=0.0`.

| job | model / path | harness | score | latency s | output tok/s | note |
|---:|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| 1809755 | `nvidia/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4` on current PR head `9adcb9ba6` after moving dispatcher config to `unquant.py` | 5-shot, max_tokens=16384 | 0.974886 | 764.946 | 3732.387 | pass; post-review validation using `dispatcher_output_dtype=bf16` from `UnquantizedFusedMoEMethod.process_weights_after_loading` |
| 1809166 | `nvidia/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4` on previous PR head `87028f590` after merging #22822 | 5-shot, max_tokens=16384 | 0.974125 | 928.865 | 3066.963 | pass; post-#22822 validation using `dispatcher_output_dtype=bf16` |
| 1807077 | `nvidia/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4` on cleanup logic, validated commit `431eecc16` | 5-shot, max_tokens=16384 | 0.979452 | 1014.345 | 2868.178 | pass; pre-#22822 validation of the same unquantized DeepGEMM routing |
| 1763935 | `Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8` baseline-like reference | 5-shot, max_tokens=16384 | 0.976408 | 291.452 | 11812.895 | comparable accuracy |

## Speed Tests and Profiling

Setup:
- Model: `nvidia/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4`
- Workload: random 1k ISL / 1k OSL, c=4..512, `max-concurrent-requests=512`
- GPU layout: total 12 GPUs, decode 8 GPUs
- DeepGEMM follow-up job: `1802777` (pre-#22822 validation of the same unquantized DeepGEMM routing)
- Previous local PyTorch fallback baseline job: `1806476` (`HEAD^` before removing the local fallback)
- DeepGEMM BF16 marker was observed in the DeepGEMM logs:
  - `<GROUPED_GEMM_NT_BF16_MASKED> N=2048, K=4096, num_groups=64`
  - `<GROUPED_GEMM_NT_BF16_MASKED> N=4096, K=1024, num_groups=64`
- The previous local fallback baseline had no `<GROUPED_GEMM_NT_BF16_MASKED>` marker.

| concurrency | local fallback output tok/s | DeepGEMM output tok/s | DeepGEMM delta | local fallback median TPOT ms | DeepGEMM median TPOT ms | local fallback median TTFT ms | DeepGEMM median TTFT ms |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 4 | 74.711 | 71.369 | -4.5% | 47.943 | 49.180 | 421.863 | 423.136 |
| 8 | 151.433 | 155.797 | +2.9% | 46.639 | 45.198 | 415.476 | 415.969 |
| 16 | 292.595 | 330.539 | +13.0% | 46.277 | 44.012 | 413.654 | 383.009 |
| 32 | 541.348 | 579.363 | +7.0% | 45.982 | 44.034 | 411.435 | 383.484 |
| 64 | 1144.301 | 1137.058 | -0.6% | 47.209 | 44.235 | 416.123 | 382.408 |
| 128 | 2207.457 | 2267.953 | +2.7% | 47.389 | 45.045 | 424.436 | 469.087 |
| 256 | 4275.715 | 4539.305 | +6.2% | 47.795 | 44.762 | 556.058 | 626.491 |
| 512 | 8239.496 | 8965.792 | +8.8% | 48.958 | 45.313 | 1125.183 | 1169.896 |

Interpretation: the DeepGEMM path is comparable to slightly better overall. It is neutral/noisy at c=4 and c=64, and better at c=8/16/32/128/256/512. The high-concurrency points, especially c=256 and c=512, are improved by +6.2% and +8.8% output throughput.

## Checklist

- [ ] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit).
- [ ] Add unit tests according to the [Run and add unit tests](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#run-and-add-unit-tests).
- [ ] Update documentation according to [Write documentations](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#write-documentations).
- [x] Provide accuracy and speed benchmark results according to [Test the accuracy](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#test-the-accuracy) and [Benchmark the speed](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#benchmark-the-speed).
- [x] Follow the SGLang code style [guidance](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-style-guidance).

## Review and Merge Process

1. Ping Merge Oncalls to start the process. See the [PR Merge Process](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/.github/MAINTAINER.md#pull-request-merge-process).
2. Get approvals from [CODEOWNERS](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/.github/CODEOWNERS) and other reviewers.
3. Trigger CI tests with [comments](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#how-to-trigger-ci-tests) or contact authorized users to do so.
   - Common commands include `/tag-and-rerun-ci`, `/tag-run-ci-label`, `/rerun-failed-ci`
4. After green CI and required approvals, ask Merge Oncalls or people with Write permission to merge the PR.

repo.name: sglang
repo.description: SGLang is a fast serving framework for large language models and vision language models.

repo.name: sglang
repo.description: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models.

files.patch:
@@ -4,7 +4,6 @@
 from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Dict, Optional, Union
 
 import torch
-import torch.nn.functional as F
 
 from sglang.srt.compilation.piecewise_context_manager import is_in_piecewise_cuda_graph
 from sglang.srt.environ import envs
@@ -102,6 +101,19 @@ def __init__(
             and quant_config.get_name() == "modelopt_fp4"
         ):
             self.deprecate_flag = True
+        elif (
+            quant_config is None
+            and self.w13_weight.dtype == torch.bfloat16
+            and get_moe_runner_backend().is_deep_gemm()
+            and get_moe_a2a_backend().is_deepep()
+            and get_deepep_mode().enable_low_latency()
+            and not _is_npu
+            and not _is_hip
+        ):
+            assert (
+                deep_gemm_wrapper.ENABLE_JIT_DEEPGEMM
+            ), "Unquantized DeepEP low-latency MoE requires DeepGEMM BF16"
+            self.deprecate_flag = True
         else:
             self.deprecate_flag = False
 
@@ -123,23 +135,13 @@ def __init__(
             self.use_block_quant = False
